sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 99, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: After RIGHT, bar moved to cols 32-35 (left of player). White block is on RIGHT (col 39), not adjacent to bar. SPACE didn't expand because no object on right. Goal hypothesis: Eat the bar by expanding white block over it. Need white block on LEFT side adjacent to bar.
- Goal model: Navigate the player to interact with the targets. Likely need to reach the center target first.
- Plan: Move LEFT so white block is adjacent to bar, then SPACE to eat it.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 98 actions; a strong score needs about 71 or fewer. You are 1.4x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After LEFT x2:
- Player: white at col 32, green at cols 33-35
- Bar: blue at cols 36-39, gray at cols 36-38
The bar is now at cols 36-39, which is to the RIGHT of the player. The white block is at col 32, on the LEFT side of the player. The bar is NOT adjacent to the white block.
Wait, the bar moved AGAIN! It was at cols 32-35 before, and now it's at cols 36-39. The player moved LEFT, and the bar moved RIGHT. They're moving in opposite directions.
I think the bar is actually a separate entity that follows or mirrors the player's movement. Or maybe the bar is the "center target" and it's moving with the player.
Actually, looking at the positions:
- Player green: cols 33-35
- Bar: cols 36-39
The bar is always 1 column to the right of the player's green base. When the player moves RIGHT, the bar moves LEFT. When the player moves LEFT, the bar moves RIGHT. They're maintaining a fixed distance.
Wait, that doesn't make sense either. Let me trace the positions:
1. Initial: player at cols 40-43, bar at cols 32-39
2. After LEFT x5 + DOWN: player at cols 33-35, bar at cols 32-39 (unchanged)
3. After SPACE (expand LEFT): bar at cols 36-39 (shrunk from left)
4. After SPACE (shrink): bar at cols 36-39 (unchanged)
5. After RIGHT: player at cols 36-38, bar at cols 32-35 (moved left)
6. After SPACE (no expand): bar at cols 32-35 (unchanged)
7. After LEFT x2: player at cols 33-35, bar at cols 36-39 (moved right)
So the bar moves when the player moves, but only after the bar was "eaten" (shrunk). Before eating, the bar was stationary. After eating, the bar moves in the opposite direction of the player.
Actually, I think the bar is the "center target" that was converted. When I ate part of it, it became a "sliding" object. Or maybe the bar is a separate mechanic.
Let me reconsider the game. The bar has:
- Blue frame (b) at rows 28-31, cols 36-39
- Gray interior (g) at rows 29-30, cols 36-38
This looks like a horizontal version of the targets. The targets are vertical rectangles with charcoal frames and blue centers. The bar is a horizontal rectangle with blue frame and gray interior.
Maybe the bar is a "converted" target that was rotated? Or maybe it's a different type of object.
I've been going in circles. Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the goal is to:
1. Convert all three targets to dark gray
2. Have the player in a specific position
3. Press SPACE to complete the level
But the targets revert when the player moves away. So I can't have all three converted simultaneously.
Unless... the player can be in a position where all three targets are adjacent? Let me check the target positions:
- Left target: rows 32-35, cols 16-19
- Center target: rows 28-31, cols 28-31
- Top-right target: rows 24-27, cols 44-47
These are all far apart. The player can't be adjacent to all three at once.
Let me try another hypothesis. Maybe the goal is to:
1. Move the player to each target
2. Press SPACE to "collect" the target
3. The collected targets are stored in the bar
4. When all three targets are collected, the level completes
But I don't see any change in the bar when I collect targets. The bar only changes when I eat it with the white block.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the bar's structure. The bar has:
- Blue frame (b) at rows 28-31, cols 36-39
- Gray interior (g) at rows 29-30, cols 36-38
The gray interior is 2x3 = 6 pixels. The blue frame is 4x4 - 2x3 = 10 pixels (but the output says 10 pixels, which is 4 rows x 4 cols - 6 interior = 10). So the bar is a 4x4 object with a 2x3 gray interior.
The targets are 4x4 objects with 2x2 blue centers. So the bar is similar to the targets but with a different interior.
Maybe the bar is a "slot" where collected targets go. When I collect a target, it appears in the bar. But the bar's interior is gray, not blue or dark gray.
I'm stuck. Let me try pressing SPACE at the current position to see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at cols 32-35, bar at cols 36-39 (right of player). White block at col 32 (left of player). Bar moves opposite to player after being eaten.
Goal hypothesis: Unclear. Need to try SPACE to see if it does something different now.
Plan: Try SPACE at current position to see if white block expands into bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try SPACE - white block is on left, bar is on right
# SPACE might expand left (away from bar) or do nothing
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"After SPACE: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 24 and max(rows) <= 40:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try SPACE again
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter 2nd SPACE: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 24 and max(rows) <= 40:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
